mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates recovering manufacturing output, which raises corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Falsified if positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative twenty-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty-five percent of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below fifty basis points, the zero lower bound forces investors out of cash and into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Falsified if S&P 500 sixty-day forward returns are negative in more than half of the months where FEDFUNDS registers below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production signal a coordinated recovery in demand and supply, amplifying earnings growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Falsified if the joint positive signal from RSAFS and INDPRO does not produce positive twenty-day returns in at least sixty percent of occurrences during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal a bottoming in residential investment, which precedes broader credit easing and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Falsified if positive month-over-month changes in HOUST during post-shock recovery periods are followed by negative sixty-day S&P 500 returns in the majority of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding twenty basis points signals rapid labor market deterioration that compresses consumer spending forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Falsified if the five-day forward return is positive in more than fifty percent of instances where the monthly UNRATE increase exceeds 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Joint declines in the federal funds rate and concurrent rises in consumer prices generate negative real interest rates, which reflate asset values and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Falsified if the combined signal of falling FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL fails to generate positive sixty-day returns in more than fifty-five percent of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding one percent indicates a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Falsified if five-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than half of the months when INDPRO posts a greater than one percent monthly decline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in payroll employment and housing starts confirm a durable recovery in labor income and construction activity, which jointly boost aggregate demand and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Falsified if the joint positive signal from PAYEMS and HOUST produces negative twenty-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty percent of walk-forward instances.
